role_title: Supply Chain Program Manager SCPM - AI Infrastructure
secondary_archetype: Supply Chain / Hardware Operations Program Manager

seniority.level: Senior IC (likely Senior or Staff TPM)
level_signals: 8+ years of experience in Supply Chain Program Management or related roles
Compensation range $226K-$285K USD consistent with Senior/Staff IC at OpenAI
Expected to lead executive-level program updates and establish governance models

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Bachelor's degree in Supply Chain, Engineering, Operations, Business, or related field.
8+ years of experience in Supply Chain Program Management, Material Program Management, Commodity Management, Strategic Sourcing, or related hardware supply chain roles.
Experience supporting complex hardware products through NPI and production ramps.
Strong understanding of hardware supply chains, material planning, and supplier operations.
Experience managing constrained supply environments and long lead-time commodities.
Experience working with ODMs, CMs, and global supplier ecosystems.
Strong analytical, program management, and executive communication skills.
Ability to drive cross-functional alignment in ambiguous and fast-moving environments.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience supporting AI/ML infrastructure, servers, networking, storage, or custom silicon programs.
Experience with ASIC supply chains, advanced packaging, HBM/DDR memory ecosystems, or hyperscale infrastructure deployments.
Familiarity with ERP/MRP/PLM systems, BOM structuring, and supply planning tools.
Experience building supply resiliency and second-source strategies.
Experience operating in highly constrained and rapidly scaling environments.

scope.surface: Supply chain execution for AI infrastructure hardware programs including custom silicon, systems, memory, storage, networking, and rack infrastructure
primary_metrics_inferred: Material readiness against NPI and production schedules
Supply shortage and allocation risk reduction
Supply lead time and inventory health KPIs
On-time delivery for proto/EVT/DVT/PVT/mass production builds
core_responsibilities: Own end-to-end material readiness across NPI and production phases, including building the necessary framework and processes for enablement
Drive supply planning and execution for long lead-time and constrained commodities including ASICs, HBM, DDR, SSDs, networking, optics, power, thermal, and mechanicals
Build and manage material readiness plans aligned to proto/pre-EVT, EVT, DVT, PVT, and mass production schedules
Monitor supply health, lead times, inventory positions, allocation risk, and capacity constraints
Drive shortage management, allocation mitigation, and recovery planning
Partner with Engineering to translate product requirements into executable supply chain strategies
Lead material reviews, supply risk assessments, and executive-level program updates
Establish governance models and operating cadences across internal and external stakeholders
Drive supplier accountability around lead times, commits, recovery plans, and escalation management
Build dashboards and KPI frameworks for supply readiness, shortages, inventory exposure, and operational health
Drive continuous improvement initiatives across supply chain execution and operational processes

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and competitive ($226K-$285K base)
Clear scope and ownership area around AI infrastructure hardware supply chain
Detailed responsibilities indicate a well-defined role with executive visibility
Role tied to OpenAI's proprietary silicon program (Jalapeño and successors) — high-impact work

Export control language ('candidates for this role may need to meet certain legal status requirements') implies potential US-person requirement but is not explicitly stated — ambiguous work authorization requirements
Equity not mentioned despite competitive base range — total comp picture incomplete
